description_long: This object is a vintage CRT computer monitor with a boxy, rectangular shape and a slightly protruding, curved glass screen. The casing is a muted beige color, typical of older electronics, and appears to be made of sturdy plastic. The front features a thick bezel framing the dark display area, with two rectangular buttons or controls located along the bottom edge. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, while the rear has a small rectangular vent or port. The monitor sits on a flat, integrated base that is slightly angled, providing stability and a modest tilt to the screen. There are no visible logos or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian, retro appearance.
description: A beige, boxy vintage CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, curved glass screen, and two buttons along the bottom front edge. The design is utilitarian and retro, with a flat integrated base and no visible branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large, dark curved screen, thick beige bezel, and two rectangular buttons at the bottom edge.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the monitor's depth, smooth beige casing, and a small rectangular vent or port near the rear.
description_view_2: Top-down view highlighting the monitor's rectangular footprint and the curvature of the screen within the thick bezel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1 but without the visible vent or port, showing the plain, smooth casing.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and made of beige plastic, with slightly rounded corners and a matte finish. It frames the display area prominently and lacks any decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a flat, integrated base that is slightly angled, made of the same beige plastic as the casing, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: Two rectangular buttons are located along the bottom edge of the front bezel, grouped together and easily accessible.
